mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and elevated recession probability, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no statistically significant difference in 60-day forward returns compared to baseline (p > 0.05), or interaction term coefficient is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 8.0 percent, equity markets price in an imminent Federal Reserve policy pause, compressing rate hike expectations and generating positive short-term returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during high inflation periods are negative or flat, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation remains above threshold for > 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect stronger economic output and manufacturing activity, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with industrial production levels.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates robust labor demand without overheating, supporting economic resilience and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields a negative or zero hit rate over 20-day horizons, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from independent proxy effects.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals economic contraction and weakening corporate fundamentals, causing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during low GDP growth periods are positive in > 60% of cases, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated policy rates immediately increase borrowing costs and compress growth stock valuations, producing negative short-term equity returns across the S&P 500.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative correlation with federal funds rate levels, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
